description: The long run continues. Springfield keeps evolving with the times.

episodes.title: Homer the Whopper
episodes.description: Homer gets the lead in a movie about a superhero who can take on the powers of every superhero in any comic book he touches. To whip Homer into superhero shape, the studio hires a celebrity fitness trainer.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Bank Teller / Writer #4 (voice)

episodes.parody_of: superhero movies and Hollywood fitness culture

episodes.title: Bart Gets a 'Z'
episodes.description: The fourth grade students of Springfield Elementary School decide to spike Ms. Krabappel's coffee in order to teach her a lesson after she takes away their cell phones. She is fired by Principal Skinner, who hires a new teacher named Zachary Vaughn. Although Vaughn is a hip young teacher who impresses the students, Bart is plagued by guilt and tries to get Ms. Krabappel hired back.

episodes.title: The Great Wife Hope
episodes.description: The men and boys in Springfield become obsessed with Ultimate Punch Kick and Choke Championships, a violent sport where fighters inflict severe pain on each other. When Marge and her friends stage a protest against the sport, the show's promoter embraces the controversy and challenges Marge to a match, promising to shut down the competition if she wins. She begins to train with rhythmic gymnastics, but her family step in and get her professional martial arts help to give her a real chance at winning.

guests.character: Crazy Cat Lady / Bernice Hibbert / Dolph (voice)
Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Sarah Wiggum (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the first major animated TV parodies of the then-exploding UFC phenomenon, airing just months after UFC 100 broke pay-per-view records and brought mixed martial arts into mainstream American culture.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XX
episodes.description:
Dial "M" for Murder or Press "#" to Return to Main Menu- Lisa is forced into a Hitchcockian murder scheme by Bart.

Don't Have a Cow, Mankind- Springfield is once again overrun by zombies, thanks to Krusty Burger's latest sandwich.

There's No Business Like Moe Business- In a Sweeney Todd spoof, Moe bleeds Homer dry to create the perfect microbrewed great beer.

guests.character: Jimbo Jones / Dracula's Wife / Rod Flanders (voice)
Wolfman's Wife / Miss Hoover / Helen Lovejoy (voice)
Dolph / Agnes Skinner / Mrs Muntz / The Mummy's Wife (voice)

episodes.parody_of: Alfred Hitchcock films, zombie movies, Sweeney Todd
episodes.notes: This marked the twentieth Treehouse of Horror episode, making it the longest-running horror anthology segment in animated television history.

episodes.title: The Devil Wears Nada
episodes.description: Marge and a group called the "Charity Chicks" pose for a calendar in hopes of raising money for charity, but Marge becomes the talk of the town thanks to her racy poses. Meanwhile, Carl is chosen as the newest supervisor at the nuclear power plant, and hires Homer to be his personal assistant.

guests.character: Bernice Hibbert / Agnes Skinner / Carla Bruni-Sarkozy (voice)
Milhouse Van Houten / Sarah Wiggum / Rod Flanders (voice)

episodes.title: Pranks and Greens
episodes.description: Principal Skinner is getting fed up with Bart's pranks, so he informs Bart that he is not the best prankster after all. A former student named Andy Hamilton is hailed as the best prankster, and Bart sets out on a mission to track Andy down. However, when Bart finds out that Andy is a 19-year-old still in his pranking days, they become fast friends.

episodes.title: Rednecks and Broomsticks
episodes.description: Lisa befriends three teenaged Wiccans after getting lost in the woods during a game of hide-and-seek, and must clear her new friends' names when they are accused of cursing the townspeople with their supposed witchcraft. Meanwhile, Homer befriends Cletus after learning that he and his friends make their own moonshine.
episodes.parody_of: The Blair Witch Project and rural/redneck stereotypes
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the few times Lisa's religious exploration extends beyond Buddhism, as she temporarily embraces Wiccan practices before ultimately rejecting them. The Blair Witch Project parody elements made it one of the more contemporary pop culture references for Season 21, though the film was already a decade old by the episode's air date.

episodes.title: Oh Brother, Where Bart Thou?
episodes.description: One snowy day in Springfield, Lisa informs Bart that she and Maggie share a bond that Bart will never understand because he doesn't have a brother, so Bart asks Homer for a baby brother. When Homer denies Bart's request, Bart makes his way to the Springfield Orphanage to find what he thinks he's missing.

guests.character: Dolph / Agnes Skinner / Adult Maggie Simpson (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marks the first time in the series that Bart actively seeks out a sibling relationship, exploring his role as the only Simpson son through his visit to the Springfield Orphanage.

episodes.title: Thursdays with Abie
episodes.description: Grampa is sitting on a bench waiting for his family when a thoughtful-looking man named Marshall Goldman approaches him and asks to hear about his life. Grampa tells Goldman about the time he was on a World War II battleship, which was hit by an enemy torpedo, and Goldman, a human-interest columnist, publishes it in The Springfield Shopper. Later, Grampa meets Mitch Albom and shares another story with Goldman for a follow-up article. Homer becomes jealous of Grampa's newfound fame and seeks a surrogate father. But when Homer discovers a draft of Goldman's third story, he must race to find Grampa before it is published and his life is changed forever.
episodes.notes: Features the real Mitch Albom as himself, author of the bestselling memoir "Tuesdays with Morrie" that this episode parodies.

episodes.title: Once Upon a Time in Springfield
episodes.description: Krusty grudgingly adds a pretty, singing cohost named Princess Penelope to his show in order to attract more female viewers. Girls love her, and ratings improve, but Bart and Milhouse want to get the show back to the way it was. The situation gets more complicated when Krusty and Penelope fall in love and decide to get married. Meanwhile, Homer, Lenny, and Carl consider working at a competing nuclear plant when Mr. Burns cuts off their daily doughnut service.
episodes.notes: Anne Hathaway guest starred as Princess Penelope, marking one of her few animated voice acting roles during her rise to A-list stardom.

episodes.title: Million Dollar Maybe
episodes.description: Homer blows off Marge to buy a lottery ticket and winds up winning the million-dollar jackpot. Fearing how Marge will react if she finds out the reason Homer missed their date, Homer keeps his newly inherited fortune a secret and spoils his family with “anonymous” gifts. When Bart finds out, he and Homer spend the money like it’s going out of style, including buying front-row to tickets to see Coldplay in concert.

episodes.title: Boy Meets Curl
episodes.description: Homer takes Marge out for a romantic evening of ice skating and hand-holding, but upon entering the rink, they encounter a curling team practising. Marge and Homer take to the ice and discover their love for the sport, and soon after, join the curling team and compete with them in the Olympic trials. Team Springfield claims the win and moves on to the 2010 Vancouver Games where Bob Costas covers the action. Meanwhile, sleazy vendors introduce Lisa to the world of collecting Olympic pins, and before long, Lisa is hopelessly addicted.
episodes.notes: Real NBC sportscaster Bob Costas guest starred as himself, providing Olympic commentary for Team Springfield's curling matches at the 2010 Vancouver Winter Olympics.

episodes.title: The Color Yellow
episodes.description: When Miss Hoover asks her students to research their family history, Lisa is horrified to discover that most of her ancestors were bad people – a motley crew of horse thieves and deadbeats. But while rummaging through the attic, Lisa happens upon a diary kept by her ancestor, Eliza Simpson. As Eliza’s story unfolds, Lisa learns that her family was part of the Underground Railroad, a group that helped slaves escape to freedom. Eliza recounts liberating a slave named Virgil, but when Lisa presents her findings at school, some of her classmates refute it, leaving Lisa determined to exonerate her family’s name.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Milford Van Houten (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the few times The Simpsons directly addressed slavery and the Underground Railroad, making it one of the show's most serious historical explorations outside of anthology segments.

episodes.title: Postcards from the Wedge
episodes.description: When Bart fails to turn in his homework, Mrs. Krabappel sends a letter home about Bart's behaviour. Despite Bart's best efforts to intercept it, Homer reads the letter, and he and Marge visit Principal Skinner for a parent-teacher conference. Furious, Homer punishes him, but Marge takes a more sympathetic approach. When Bart realizes he can pit Homer and Marge against each other to his benefit, his scheming reaches new heights.

episodes.title: Stealing First Base
episodes.description: Principal Skinner announces that Mrs. Krabappel was called out of town and budget cuts dictate that, until she returns, the school's two fourth grade classes will merge. Bart reluctantly shares a desk with Nikki and develops a flirtatious rapport. Bart talks to Grampa about his new crush, and at Grampa's suggestion, gives Nikki a kiss. But when Nikki starts sending Bart mixed signals, he swears off women forever. Meanwhile, Lisa's classmates ostracize her for being an overachiever, and First Lady Michelle Obama, a self-professed nerd, comes to Lisa's defence.
episodes.notes: First Lady Michelle Obama guest starred as herself, making this one of the rare times a sitting First Lady appeared on The Simpsons.

episodes.title: The Greatest Story Ever D'ohed
episodes.description: The Simpsons family vacation in Israel with Ned Flanders, but Homer does not appreciate any of the culture—until an Israeli tour guide named Jakob shows him around and, in a severe state of dehydration, Homer believes he is the Messiah.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the few times The Simpsons directly engaged with Middle Eastern politics and religion, airing during a period of heightened sensitivity around depictions of religious figures in animated television.

episodes.title: American History X-cellent
episodes.description: When the police are called to diffuse a rowdy crowd at Mr. Burns' estate, one of the officers recognizes priceless stolen paintings on the walls, and the maniacal billionaire is taken downtown for questioning. With Mr. Burns gone, Smithers takes charge of the power plant. But when employees take advantage of his good nature, he exacts revenge by forcing the employees to work night and day. These unfair working conditions prompt Homer and his crew to devise a plan to bust out Mr. Burns.
episodes.parody_of: Prison break films and TV shows

episodes.title: Chief of Hearts
episodes.description: Homer is completing his court-ordered community service when he befriends his supervisor, Chief Wiggum, by offering him one of his sandwiches. Touched by the act of kindness, Wiggum assigns the other convicts unpleasant tasks, but allows Homer to join him at the picnic table. They continue to grow close, but when the Chief gets injured during a botched bank robbery, Homer doesn't come through when Wiggum needs him the most. Meanwhile, Bart becomes addicted to Battle Ball, a Japanese game made up of plastic balls and magnetic cards, and his family and teachers try to help him kick the habit.

guests.character: 'He's got a gun!' / Milhouse Van Houten / Sarah Wiggum (voice)
Cookie Kwan / Sassy Black Woman / Pigs (voice)

episodes.title: The Squirt and the Whale
episodes.description: The Simpsons decide to embrace a cheaper, alternate source of energy by erecting a wind turbine in their backyard. But when Homer realizes some of the power is being directed to the local electric company, he decides to remove his home from the grid and becomes completely dependent upon an unreliable source of power. Meanwhile, a storm erupts, trapping a magnificent 150-foot-long blue whale ashore, and Lisa and Homer attempt to help the poor creature.

episodes.title: To Surveil with Love
episodes.description: A bomb squad mistakenly blows up Homer's unattended gym bag, releasing radiation into the city and authorities react by suspending civil liberties. Wiggum and his men install surveillance cameras around Springfield and round up suspected terrorists, including groundskeeper Willie, but when monitoring the nonstop flow of video imagery proves to be too much, Wiggum enlists concerned citizens to help keep the city safe. Meanwhile, Lisa becomes fed up with being blonde, so she dyes her hair a dark color.
episodes.parody_of: post-9/11 surveillance state and security theater

episodes.title: Moe Letter Blues
episodes.description: As Mother's Day approaches, Moe narrates an episode in which he writes a letter to Homer, Apu and Reverend Lovejoy, who are vacationing with their children, and threatens to run away with one of their wives. While the trio tries to determine whose wife Moe is referring to, Homer, Lovejoy and Apu flashback to the intimate moments they initially ignored between Moe and Marge, Manjula and Helen Lovejoy. But when the boys return from their trip, they're in for the surprise of their lives.

episodes.title: The Bob Next Door
episodes.description: Bart becomes convinced that his new neighbor, Walt, is his archenemy, Sideshow Bob, disguised and back for revenge. But when Marge tries to convince Bart otherwise by taking him to visit the state penitentiary, a disturbing truth is revealed.
episodes.notes: This episode features the most elaborate Sideshow Bob plot twist in the series, revealing that Bob surgically switched faces with his cellmate Walt Warren to escape prison. The face-swap concept pushed the show's cartoon logic to new extremes, even by Sideshow Bob episode standards.

episodes.title: Judge Me Tender
episodes.description: When Moe discovers a hidden talent for judging contests, he is approached by a television agent to join the AMERICAN IDOL judges' panel. Moe flies to Los Angeles where he tours the Fox lot and receives some sage career advice. Meanwhile, Homer drives Marge crazy when he begins hanging around the house.
episodes.notes: This episode featured actual American Idol judge Randy Jackson as himself, marking one of the few times a current reality TV judge appeared on The Simpsons during the show's peak parody period.

episodes.title: Elementary School Musical
episodes.description: Marge sends Lisa to a performing arts camp, where counselors and fellow campers inspire her to embrace her creative side; upon returning to Springfield, Lisa grows restless with her boring suburban lifestyle.
episodes.notes: This Season 22 premiere marked The Simpsons' entry into the musical comedy trend popularized by High School Musical and Glee, featuring elaborate song-and-dance numbers that parodied the Disney Channel phenomenon.

episodes.title: Loan-a-Lisa
episodes.description: Grampa gives each family member a portion of his savings; Lisa worries that Nelson's instant success with a new business venture might lure him away from continuing his education.

episodes.title: MoneyBART
episodes.description: Lisa starts coaching Bart's Little League team; Marge and Bart spend the day at an amusement park, where former MLB manager Mike Scioscia gives Bart advice.
episodes.notes: Mike Scioscia guest starred as himself, marking one of the few times a real MLB manager appeared on The Simpsons to give actual baseball advice rather than serve as comedic fodder.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XXI
episodes.description: Worried about the effects of violent video games, Marge encourages Bart and Milhouse to play board games; Lisa becomes smitten with a new student, a vampire; while on a second honeymoon, Homer and Marge rescue a castaway who escaped from his ship.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as zombies, with their flesh falling off.
episodes.parody_of: Jumanji, Twilight, The Diving Bell and the Butterfly
episodes.notes: The Twilight parody segment "Tweenlight" aired at the height of vampire romance popularity, making it one of the most culturally timely Treehouse of Horror segments ever produced.

episodes.title: Lisa Simpson, This Isn't Your Life
episodes.description: Fearing that she will end up like Marge, Lisa pledges to focus solely on her academics and not get distracted by other things; Bart puts Nelson in his place and unintentionally earns the title of school bully.

episodes.title: The Fool Monty
episodes.description: Mr. Burns becomes distraught at the town's apathetic reaction to the announcement that he has only weeks to live; Bart secretly takes Burns into the family's home.

episodes.title: How Munched Is That Birdie in the Window?
episodes.description: After Bart nurses an injured bird back to health, Homer enters it in a race.

episodes.title: The Fight Before Christmas
episodes.description: When the rest of the family seems to lack Christmas spirit, Marge contacts Martha Clause.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, then the camera pulls back to reveal they're inside a snow globe being shaken by Santa's hands.
episodes.parody_of: The Nutcracker and various Christmas specials
episodes.notes: This anthology-style Christmas special features four distinct animation styles including stop-motion puppetry and anime sequences, making it one of the most visually experimental episodes in the series' history.

episodes.title: Donnie Fatso
episodes.description: Homer ends up in the Springfield penitentiary after getting caught bribing an official; an FBI investigator offers Homer a way to reduce his jail time.
episodes.parody_of: Mafia/organized crime films and FBI informant stories

episodes.title: Moms I'd Like to Forget
episodes.description: Marge reconnects with a close group of mothers she used to be friends with; Bart tries to break up their group when his playdates stop being fun.

episodes.title: Flaming Moe
episodes.description: When Smithers transforms Moe's pub into a refined and trendy lounge, the establishment becomes Springfield's local gay hangout; Principal Skinner falls for the new, free-spirited music teacher.

episodes.title: Homer the Father
episodes.description: When Homer insists Bart must earn the new dirt bike he wants, Bart comes up with a scheme; James Lipton interviews a TV cast.
episodes.parody_of: Leave It to Beaver and 1950s family sitcoms
episodes.notes: James Lipton appears as himself interviewing the fictional 1950s sitcom family from "Leave It to Beaver," marking one of the few times a real TV personality has appeared on The Simpsons to discuss fictional characters from another show.

episodes.title: The Blue and the Gray
episodes.description: Moe attends a seminar in hope of gaining more confidence with women; Marge makes a change after discovering her gray hair.

episodes.title: Angry Dad: The Movie
episodes.description: Bart's film about an angry father quickly becomes a critical favorite, winning him several awards.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets sucked into the TV, where they appear in various shows.
episodes.parody_of: Independent filmmaking and award season culture
episodes.notes: Bart's animated web series "Angry Dad" becomes the first internet-based creation to win an Academy Award in the Simpsons universe, marking the show's prescient commentary on digital content competing with traditional Hollywood filmmaking.

episodes.title: The Scorpion's Tale
episodes.description: During a field trip, Lisa discovers desert water that makes usually combative creatures get along; after getting kicked out of the retirement home, Grampa comes to live with the family.

guests.character: Agnes Skinner / Mrs. Fornby / Flight Attendent (voice)

episodes.title: A Midsummer's Nice Dream
episodes.description: When Cheech and Chong stop in Springfield while on their reunion tour, Homer impresses Cheech so much he asks him to replace Chong; Principal Skinner forms a new comedy team with Chong; Marge tries to help a hoarder.
episodes.notes: Cheech and Chong appeared as themselves in their first animated TV guest roles together, marking a rare instance of the legendary comedy duo performing scripted material outside their standup acts.

episodes.title: Love Is a Many Strangled Thing
episodes.description: Marge persuades Homer to enroll in a fathering enrichment class; Bart becomes a school bully.

guests.character: Security Guard / Burns' Cellmate / Various Voices (voice)

episodes.title: The Great Simpsina
episodes.description: When the Great Raymondo takes Lisa on as his magician's assistant, she is conned into revealing the secret of his greatest trick to his nemesis.

episodes.title: The Real Housewives of Fat Tony
episodes.description: Fat Tony and Marge's sister, Selma, fall in love and get married; Bart discovers a special ability.
episodes.parody_of: The Real Housewives reality TV franchise
episodes.notes: Marks one of the few times a Bouvier sister gets married on screen, with Selma's union to Fat Tony lasting only one episode before his death.

episodes.title: Homer Scissorhands
episodes.description: Homer opens his own hair salon after discovering he is a talented stylist; Milhouse professes his love for Lisa.

guests.character: Lindsay Naegle / Manjula / Lunchlady Doris / Cookie Kwan (voice)

episodes.title: 500 Keys
episodes.description: After finding the keys to every door in Springfield, Lisa finds a hidden room beneath the elementary school.

episodes.title: The Ned-liest Catch
episodes.description: When Ned starts dating Mrs. Krabappel, he is surprised to learn how many of Springfield's men she has been with.
episodes.notes: This episode establishes the romantic relationship between Ned Flanders and Edna Krabappel that would continue throughout the series until Marcia Wallace's death in 2013. The storyline was partly inspired by fan speculation about these two widowed characters finding companionship together.

episodes.title: The Falcon and the D'ohman
episodes.description: Homer befriends a new security guard at the plant; Marge daydreams about being a contestant on "Top Chef."
episodes.notes: This Season 23 premiere marked the show's transition to HD production after 22 seasons in standard definition, fundamentally changing The Simpsons' visual presentation for the modern era.

episodes.title: Bart Stops to Smell the Roosevelts
episodes.description: When superintendent Chalmers is fired for taking Bart and his friends on an unauthorized field trip, the boys hold the school hostage until he is reinstated.
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the few times Superintendent Chalmers receives a sympathetic portrayal and serves as the central heroic figure, departing from his usual role as Skinner's demanding superior.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XXII
episodes.description: A boulder traps Homer's arm when he takes a drive through a canyon; a spider bite paralyzes Homer; Flanders becomes a vigilante by night; Bart and Milhouse travel to a distant planet to obtain an extract.
episodes.notes: The "Avatar" parody segment marks one of the fastest movie-to-Simpsons parodies in the show's history, airing just under two years after James Cameron's film hit theaters.

episodes.title: Replaceable You
episodes.description: Lisa's science fair project is overshadowed by Bart's mechanical baby seal; Homer's new assistant ends up stealing his job.

episodes.title: The Food Wife
episodes.description: Marge, Bart and Lisa start their own food blog and land a reservation at a popular restaurant; when Marge sends Homer to a cooking lab, he finds himself in a situation with law enforcement.
episodes.parody_of: food blogging culture and celebrity chef shows
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the earliest mainstream TV parodies of food blogging culture, predating the peak of Instagram food culture by several years.

episodes.title: The Book Job
episodes.description: Homer creates a team, including author Neil Gaiman, to write the next hit "tween" book; after receiving an advance copy of the work from his publisher, Homer learns there's more to the business than he imagined.
episodes.notes: Neil Gaiman guest starred as himself, marking one of the few times a major contemporary author appeared on The Simpsons to parody the publishing industry's formulaic approach to young adult fiction.

episodes.title: The Man in the Blue Flannel Pants
episodes.description: The Simpsons host a party to promote Krusty's own brand of liquor; impressed with the party's sucess, Mr. Burns decides to promote Homer; Bart takes an interest in reading classic novels.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Kitten in Wheelchair (voice)

episodes.title: The Ten-Per-Cent Solution
episodes.description: After Krusty the Clown's show is canceled and his talent agency drops him, his very first agent, Annie, tries to craft his comeback.

episodes.title: Holidays of Future Passed
episodes.description: Thirty years in the future, Bart, Lisa and their children spend the holidays at Marge and Homer's house.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which transforms into a sleigh that flies around Springfield before returning them to the living room.
episodes.notes: This Christmas-themed flash-forward episode became the first Simpsons episode to show the Simpson family's grandchildren, establishing Bart as having two sons and Lisa as having a daughter named Zia.

episodes.title: Politically Inept, with Homer Simpson
episodes.description: After a video of Homer ranting goes viral, he gets his own political talk show; Homer endorses rocker Ted Nugent for the Republican presidential nominee.
episodes.parody_of: Political talk shows and pundit culture
episodes.notes: Ted Nugent guest-starred as himself in one of his rare animated TV appearances, endorsing his own fictional presidential campaign alongside Homer's pundit character.

episodes.title: The D'oh-cial Network
episodes.description: Lisa is called to trial after her online social networking site creates dysfunction in the community; Patty and Selma compete in a rowing event.
episodes.notes: This marks the first time Lisa creates her own social media platform, predating real-world concerns about teen entrepreneurs and social media's impact on communities by several years.

episodes.title: Moe Goes from Rags to Riches
episodes.description: Moe's bar rag explains that he started as a medieval tapestry a thousand years earlier; Bart tries to reconcile with Milhouse; the disappearance of Moe's bar rag makes him realize he has friends.
episodes.parody_of: Forrest Gump and historical epic storytelling

episodes.title: The Daughter Also Rises
episodes.description: Marge and Lisa's plans for a mother-daughter Valentine's Day take a turn when Lisa falls head over heels for an intellectual romantic; Bart and Milhouse investigate Springfield Elementary's legends.

episodes.title: At Long Last Leave
episodes.description: When the Simpsons are kicked out of Springfield, they join an off-the-grid community near by; Homer and Marge's former friends react badly when they try to sneak back into town.
episodes.notes: This episode marked the first time in the series' history that the Simpson family was permanently exiled from Springfield, making it a rare status quo-breaking storyline that lasted multiple episodes.

episodes.title: Exit Through the Kwik-E-Mart
episodes.description: When Bart decides to get back at Homer by becoming a graffiti artist, established street artists offer him a gallery show; a new health food store threatens to put Apu out of business.
episodes.parody_of: Exit Through the Gift Shop and street art culture
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the few times The Simpsons directly parodied a contemporary documentary, spoofing Banksy's Oscar-nominated "Exit Through the Gift Shop" just two years after its release.

episodes.title: How I Wet Your Mother
episodes.description: When Homer's bed-wetting worsens, his family tries to infiltrate his dreams to find the source of the problem.
episodes.notes: The episode's dream-within-a-dream structure directly parodies Christopher Nolan's "Inception," complete with multiple dream layers and the concept of "limbo." This marked one of the most elaborate movie parodies in the series' later seasons, requiring complex animation sequences to mirror the film's shifting reality levels.

episodes.title: Them, Robot
episodes.description: When Mr. Burn replaces most of the plant's employees with robots that end up turning on the community, the people he laid off must come to the rescue.
episodes.parody_of: I, Robot and robot uprising films

episodes.title: Beware My Cheating Bart
episodes.description: Bart ends up in trouble with school bullies after Jimbo's girlfriend develops feelings for him; Homer buys a treadmill equipped with a TV.

episodes.title: A Totally Fun Thing That Bart Will Never Do Again
episodes.description: When Bart gets his wish for a family vacation, he becomes determined to make it last forever.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which turns into a boat that sails away.
episodes.parody_of: Cruise ship vacation movies and disaster films
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the rare instances where Bart successfully manipulates an entire cruise ship through elaborate deception, faking a pandemic to extend his vacation indefinitely. The cruise ship setting allowed for extensive parodies of both luxury vacation commercials and disaster movie tropes in a single episode.

episodes.title: The Spy Who Learned Me
episodes.description: Marge and Homer go on a date, but Marge is embarrassed when Homer talks during the movie; when Homer gets hurt and can't work, he tries to learn how to be a better husband from a man he believes is a movie star.

episodes.title: Ned 'n Edna's Blend Agenda
episodes.description: When Marge learns Ned and Edna got married, she offers to throw them a reception; Edna changes some rules to help Ned's kids become more socially acceptable.
episodes.notes: This episode addresses the controversial off-screen marriage of Ned Flanders and Edna Krabappel that occurred during the Season 22 finale, which many fans felt was rushed and unexplored. The reception storyline served as the writers' attempt to give proper attention to this major character development that had been largely glossed over.

episodes.title: Lisa Goes Gaga
episodes.description: Lisa tries to make herself more popular by writing good things about herself on the school blog, but the plan backfires; Lisa helps Lady Gaga and learns the importance of being yourself.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, but then Lady Gaga emerges from inside the couch wearing a dress made of cushions.
episodes.notes: Lady Gaga's appearance marked one of the most polarizing celebrity cameos in Simpsons history, with many longtime fans citing it as a symbol of the show's departure from its original spirit.

episodes.title: Moonshine River
episodes.description: Bart will be visited by many of his past girlfriends.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which turns into a car that drives through Springfield and various TV shows before returning to the living room.
episodes.notes: This season 24 premiere marked the series' transition to permanent HD production after 23 seasons in standard definition. The episode reunited Bart with multiple past love interests including Jessica Lovejoy, making it one of the few episodes to extensively callback to the show's romantic subplot history.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XXIII
episodes.description: A black hole threatens to swallow Springfield, Homer tries to find the source of paranormal activity, and a "Back to the Future" parody in which Bart keeps Marge from marrying Homer.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which turns into a monster and eats them.
episodes.notes: This marked the 24th Treehouse of Horror episode, making The Simpsons the longest-running primetime animated series to maintain an annual Halloween anthology tradition.

episodes.title: Adventures in Baby-Getting
episodes.description: When Marge's car falls into a sink hole, she buys the perfect replacement to fit her family of five. But she quickly grows to hate it because, as she tells a shocked Homer, the car would be too small if she wanted to have another baby. Meanwhile, Bart and his friends are determined to find out what secret Lisa is keeping when Bart finds her cryptic messages and follows her sneaking off downtown for covert meetings after school.

episodes.title: Gone Abie Gone
episodes.description: Homer and Marge try to track down Grampa by following clues that lead to secrets from his past.

episodes.title: Penny-Wiseguys
episodes.description: Homer is shocked to discover that his bowling teammate is an accountant for Fat Tony and his mob. When Fat Tony gets jury duty, he appoints Dan as the temporary don of the mafia. Meanwhile, Lisa adds insects to her vegetarian diet after she passes out in the middle of a saxophone solo, but starts questioning her decision when bugs start pleading for mercy in her dreams.

episodes.title: A Tree Grows in Springfield
episodes.description: Homer wins a MyPad at the school auction and Steve Mobbs, who is now in heaven, talks to Homer through it. Homer goes into a funk when he breaks the myPad, until Ned Flanders discovers a miracle tree in the Simpsons' back garden.
episodes.notes: Steve Mobbs marked one of the show's most direct celebrity parodies, airing just over a year after Steve Jobs' death in October 2011. The episode's Apple product satire came during the height of iPad popularity, making it one of the most topically relevant tech parodies in the series.

episodes.title: The Day the Earth Stood Cool
episodes.description: A "cool family" from Portland moves in next door to the Simpsons. Homer is desperate to befriend them, while Marge is skeptical, thinking that being cool is not all it's cracked up to be.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the show's most direct satires of Portland hipster culture, airing during the peak popularity of "Portlandia" and the national conversation about gentrification.

episodes.title: To Cur, with Love
episodes.description: Grampa has to move into the Simpsons house following a fire at the retirement home, and on moving day, Homer 'throws his back out' and 'rests' at home. He later finds an advert for a video game, Villageville on his myPad, and starts playing. During that time, Homer loses Santa's Little Helper. When the family find him, Homer doesn't seem to care because he's "not a dog person". Grampa later tells the rest of the family the story of Homer's childhood companion, pet dog Bongo.

episodes.title: Homer Goes to Prep School
episodes.description: Homer joins the "Springfield Preppers," an off-the-grid survivalist group whose leader has set up a top-secret retreat outside of town to prepare for the end of the world.
episodes.parody_of: Doomsday prepper/survivalist culture and shows like 'Doomsday Preppers'
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the first major network TV parodies of the doomsday prepper phenomenon, airing just one month after National Geographic's "Doomsday Preppers" reached peak popularity in late 2012.

episodes.title: A Test Before Trying
episodes.description: The fate of Springfield Elementary rests on Bart and his test scores, and Homer finds way to make money with his own parking meter.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the later instances where Bart's academic performance became central to Springfield Elementary's institutional survival, a plot device that had become increasingly rare by Season 24.

episodes.title: The Changing of the Guardian
episodes.description: A tornado touches down in Springfield which sucks up Santa's Little Helper. While Homer and Marge are out looking for the family dog, the tornado strikes again and almost takes Marge and Homer. Marge is distraught about the kids and decide to pick a  guardian to care for the children.

episodes.title: Love Is a Many-Splintered Thing
episodes.description: Bart's dream girl, Mary Spuckler, comes to town, but his failure to pay her enough attention is having a negative impact on their relationship. Meanwhile, Homer once again runs afoul of Marge and lands in the doghouse.

episodes.title: Hardly Kirk-ing
episodes.description: Bart and Milhouse explore the perks of adulthood following a shaving experiment.

episodes.title: Gorgeous Grampa
episodes.description: Homer's obsession with the TV show "Storage Battles" leads the family to discover Grampa's past identity as "Gorgeous Godfrey," the most feared, villainous man in pro-wrestling history

episodes.title: Black-Eyed, Please
episodes.description: When Flanders' parents prove to prefer Homer's company to his, he jealously responds by giving Homer a shiner, and then tries desperately to reconcile with him. Meanwhile, Lisa is at a loss to understand why a new substitute teacher is bullying her for no obvious reason.

episodes.title: Dark Knight Court
episodes.description: Springfield's Easter is ruined by a prank and all fingers point to Bart. It is up to Lisa to save her brother, in a student court presided over by Attorney General Janet Reno. Meanwhile, inspired by an old comic book, Mr. Burns becomes an ineffectual superhero—fruit bat man.
episodes.notes: Former U.S. Attorney General Janet Reno guest starred as herself, presiding over Bart's student court trial in one of the show's most unusual celebrity cameos by a government official.

episodes.title: What Animated Women Want
episodes.description: Homer's behavior once again lands him in hot water with Marge and he needs to fathom a way to save his marriage, while a dose of "A Streetcar Named Desire" drives Milhouse to affect a Marlon Brando-esque bad-boy demeanor in an effort to impress Lisa.

episodes.title: Pulpit Friction
episodes.description: Homer is recruited to be a deacon by the cutting-edge new pastor. The dry cleaner mistakenly switches Marge's wedding dress for one of Krusty's costumes.

episodes.title: Whiskey Business
episodes.description: Moe is flying high after venture capitalists take a shine to his homemade whiskey, while Grampa, after injuring himself babysitting Bart, decides he'd rather be looked after by his grandson than be at the nursing home. Also, Lisa voices her displeasure at a hologram of music legend Bleeding Gums Murphy, and Sonny Rollins (as himself) shows up to ask her to stop all that jazz about boycotting a record label.
episodes.notes: Jazz legend Sonny Rollins appears as himself, marking one of the most prestigious musical guest appearances in the show's later seasons.

episodes.title: The Fabulous Faker Boy
episodes.description: Marge encourages Bart to explore his creative side, and his initial reticence quickly subsides when she hires Slava’s beautiful daughter, Zhenya, as his new piano instructor. Homer is shocked when he loses the last two hairs on his head, and employs a wide variety of headgear to try and keep his new found baldness a secret from Marge.

episodes.title: The Saga of Carl
episodes.description: Homer, Moe, Lenny and Carl win $200,000 in the Springfield Lottery. But when Carl takes off to Iceland – his homeland – with all the winnings, the guys embark on a Nordic adventure to get the cash back.

episodes.title: Dangers on a Train
episodes.description: When Marge tries to buy Homer snack cakes for their anniversary, she winds up meeting a married man who shares a common love of British Television. Ignorant of all, Homer attempts to secretly construct a romantic anniversary train in the Simpsons back yard.
episodes.parody_of: British television shows and train romance films

episodes.title: Homerland
episodes.description: A parody of "Homeland". Homer starts to act strangely polite after returning from a nuclear power plant convention, prompting a suspicious Lisa to ask an intense FBI agent named Annie (Kristen Wiig) to help her solve this mystery.
episodes.notes: This marked the first time The Simpsons directly parodied a premium cable drama, with Kristen Wiig's FBI agent Annie Crawford serving as a clear analog to Homeland's Carrie Mathison.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XXIV
episodes.description: The scariest Simpsons Halloween show yet, featuring an opening by Guillermo Del Toro.
episodes.couch_gag: Guillermo del Toro directs an elaborate opening featuring monsters, demons, and horror movie references throughout Springfield.
episodes.parody_of: Multiple horror films and franchises including Dr. Seuss, Freaks, and Fat in the Hat
episodes.notes: Academy Award-winning director Guillermo del Toro created an elaborate opening sequence featuring dozens of horror movie references and classic monsters, marking the first time a renowned filmmaker directed content specifically for the series.

episodes.title: Four Regrettings and a Funeral
episodes.description: Kent Brockman has decided that local news has been a real mistake and a terrible career move for him.

episodes.title: YOLO
episodes.description: Marge encourages Homer to cross an item off an old to-do list by inviting an old pen pal to visit; Lisa institutes an honor code at school.

episodes.title: Labor Pains
episodes.description: When Homer helps a woman give birth in an elevator, he forms an emotional attachment to the baby; cheerleaders for the local professional football team recruit Lisa.

episodes.title: The Kid Is All Right
episodes.description: Lisa discovers her new best friend is a Republican who is opposing her in the class election.

episodes.title: Yellow Subterfuge
episodes.description: Principal Skinner promises the students that the best-behaved among them can take a ride in a submarine. When Skinner gives all the kids a clean slate, Bart believes even his own past indiscretions will be forgotten if he doesn’t get into any more trouble. Meanwhile Lisa tries to help a cash-poor Krusty to turn things around by suggesting he sell the foreign rights to his shows.

episodes.title: White Christmas Blues
episodes.description: Springfield is inundated with holiday revelers after the nuclear power plant springs a radiation leak, causing snow to fall all over town. When the tourists drive up Christmas shopping prices, Marge opens up the Simpsons' house to boarders.

episodes.title: Steal This Episode
episodes.description: Homer becomes annoyed with movie theaters, so Bart teaches him how to download movies illegally. Homer is delighted with his discovery of free movies until he's caught for piracy. Deputy Director Gratman, the FBI's anti-piracy boss, is tasked with hunting down Homer, who stands accused of illegally downloading a film.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of The Simpsons' most direct engagements with contemporary digital piracy issues, airing during the height of illegal downloading debates in the mid-2010s.

episodes.title: Married to the Blob
episodes.description: When Comic Book Guy meets a woman writing an autobiographical manga, he seeks Homer's advice on dating.
episodes.notes: Comic Book Guy finally finds love with Kumiko, a Japanese manga artist who becomes his wife and a recurring character in future episodes.

episodes.title: Specs and the City
episodes.description: When Marge tries Homer's new high-tech glasses on, he learns she's seeing a marriage counselor; Bart refuses to buy Nelson a Valentine's Day card.

guests.character: Dolph / Glasses Voice / Boy With Shades (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marked The Simpsons' first major parody of Google Glass, airing just two years after the wearable technology's initial release and during the height of public fascination with augmented reality devices.

episodes.title: Diggs
episodes.description: A recent transfer student named Diggs rescues Bart from a confrontation with bullies.

episodes.title: The Man Who Grew Too Much
episodes.description: During a research trip, Lisa learns that Sideshow Bob is the chief scientist at a big chemical engineering company; Marge tries to teach a teen church group about healthy sexual practices.
episodes.notes: This marks Sideshow Bob's first appearance in over four seasons, breaking the longest gap between his episodes since the character's introduction in 1990.

episodes.title: The Winter of His Content
episodes.description: When Retirement Castle is closed, Marge asks Grampa and two other octogenarians to move in; Nelson befriends Bart after Bart defends him.

episodes.title: The War of Art
episodes.description: After Lisa's new pet guinea pig destroys the Simpsons' living room art, Marge finds a beautiful painting at the Van Houten's yard sale, which Homer snaps up for 20 bucks

episodes.title: You Don't Have to Live Like a Referee
episodes.description: Homer is recruited as a World Cup referee after Lisa extols his integrity on the youth soccer field in a Springfield Elementary speech contest. But when the Simpsons travel to Brazil (again), Homer finds his honesty tested by one of South America's slickest gangsters in the soccer match-bribing business.
episodes.notes: This marks the Simpsons' second trip to Brazil, following their visit in "Blame It on Lisa" (2002), making Brazil one of the few countries the family has visited twice in the series.

episodes.title: Luca$
episodes.description: Lisa dates a competitive eater; Bart benefits from helping convict Snake Jailbird escape from a jam.

episodes.title: Days of Future Future
episodes.description: Set in the future, Homer has a new clone for every time he dies. Lisa is married to a zombie version of Milhouse, while Bart deals with custody issues with his ex-wife, Jenda. Each defines what love means to them.
episodes.notes: This marks the second futuristic Simpsons episode to explore the adult lives of Bart and Lisa, following the acclaimed "Lisa's Wedding" from 1995, but with a much darker sci-fi twist involving cloning and zombification.

episodes.title: What to Expect When Bart's Expecting
episodes.description: When Bart's voodoo doll spell accidentally makes his teacher pregnant, he becomes a savior to couples hoping to conceive.

episodes.title: Brick Like Me
episodes.description: Homer wakes up in a Springfield where everyone and everything is made out of Legos, and he must figure his way out before he gets stuck in the plastic world forever.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as Lego minifigures, which then breaks apart into individual Lego bricks.
episodes.notes: This marked the first time The Simpsons was entirely animated in a different style, with every character and object rendered as LEGO bricks to parody The Lego Movie.

episodes.title: Pay Pal
episodes.description: Marge swears off befriending any more couples when Homer offends their charming new British neighbors, but when Lisa declares that she, too, doesn’t need friends, Marge reconsiders.

episodes.title: The Yellow Badge of Cowardge
episodes.description: Bart feels guilty after he wins the annual "last day of school" race around Springfield Elementary School, with help from Nelson, who beats up the frontrunner, Milhouse. Meanwhile, Homer tries to bring back 4th of July fireworks after they are canceled due to budget cuts.

episodes.title: Clown in the Dumps
episodes.description: A Springfield resident dies, Krusty retires after he's offended by a comedy cable channel roast of him, and Lisa tries to protect Homer from getting hurt.

guests.character: Future Bart / Future Lisa / Future Marge (voice)

episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which is actually a monster that eats them, then spits out their skeletons.
episodes.notes: This episode marked the death of Krusty's father Rabbi Hyman Krustofsky, with Jackie Mason reprising his Emmy-winning role for the final time.

episodes.title: The Wreck of the Relationship
episodes.description: When Bart disrespects Homer's authority, Homer makes a point to try his hand at parenting. This proves fruitless, so Marge signs them up for the Relation Ship, where they'll resolve their conflicts at sea. Meanwhile, Marge is in charge of Homer's fantasy football league, but manages to win despite being the underdog.

episodes.title: Super Franchise Me
episodes.description: Marge opens a sandwich franchise store after her sandwiches become a huge hit at Springfield Elementary. But running day-to-day at the store proves challenging, especially when the same franchise opens another location across the street.
episodes.parody_of: Fast food franchise industry and business competition

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XXV
episodes.description: In a Halloween-theme episode Bart reads Aramaic symbols from the bottom of his desk and he and Lisa are transported to an alternate world filled with demons. Also, Moe organizes a "Clockwork Orange"-ish gang in which member Dum (Homer) falls for a young lady (Marge) who wants him to quit. In a homage to "The Others," the Simpsons reconnect with their earlier incarnations from the Tracey Ullman era.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which transforms into a giant monster that eats them.
episodes.parody_of: A Clockwork Orange, The Others, and general horror/supernatural themes
episodes.notes: The final segment marked the first time the original crude Tracey Ullman Show versions of the Simpson family appeared in a regular episode, serving as a meta-commentary on the show's 27-year evolution.

episodes.title: Opposites A-Frack
episodes.description: Lisa brings in assembly woman Maxine Lombard to put a stop to Mr. Burns' fracking operation, but is taken aback when the two political opponents find themselves attracted to one another.

episodes.title: Simpsorama
episodes.description: The Planet Express crew come to present-day Springfield to prevent the Simpsons from destroying the future.

guests.character: Philip J. Fry / Dr. Zoidberg / Professor Farnsworth (voice)

episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which transforms into a time machine that sends them to the future.
episodes.notes: This marked the first official crossover between The Simpsons and Futurama, with Matt Groening's two animated series sharing the screen after years of fan speculation about a potential meeting.

episodes.title: Blazed and Confused
episodes.description: Bart schemes to bring down his new teacher, Mr. Lassen, who is a terrible bully. Meanwhile, The Simpsons go to Burning Man.

episodes.title: Covercraft
episodes.description: Homer has a mid-life crisis, takes up bass guitar and forms a cover band with some of the other dads in town. But the band's modest early success is soon overwhelmed by the breakout potential of their most unlikely star, Apu.
episodes.parody_of: Rock band culture and music industry

episodes.title: I Won't Be Home for Christmas
episodes.description: After Moe convinces Homer to stay out drinking on Christmas Eve, Marge tells Homer not to come home. This leads to Homer's sad trek through lonely Springfield, where he learns that the only place where one should truly get drunk on Christmas is home.

episodes.title: The Man Who Came to Be Dinner
episodes.description: During a trip to "Dizzneeland" the Simpsons board the wrong ride, Rocket To Your Doom, and are transported through the galaxy to Kang and Kodos' home planet, where they are held captive at the local zoo and the inhabitants decide to eat Homer.
episodes.parody_of: Disney theme parks and science fiction alien abduction stories
episodes.notes: This marked the first regular-series appearance of Kang and Kodos outside of the annual Treehouse of Horror anthology episodes, breaking the show's long-standing tradition of confining the aliens to Halloween specials.

episodes.title: Bart's New Friend
episodes.description: Bart has a new accomplice and best friend when Homer is hypnotized at the circus and believes he is a young boy again.

guests.character: Howard K. Duff VIII  (voice) / Don Bookner (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode was written by Judd Apatow, marking the comedy filmmaker's only contribution to The Simpsons after previously pitching ideas to the show in the 1990s.

episodes.title: The Musk Who Fell to Earth
episodes.description: When inventor Elon Musk lands in Springfield, he and Homer become fast friends and revolutionize the town's nuclear plant. But when Musk goes overboard and Mr. Burns wants him removed, Homer must figure out a way to break up with him.
episodes.notes: Elon Musk guest starred as himself, making him one of the few real-world tech billionaires to appear on the show during the height of his public profile.

episodes.title: Walking Big & Tall
episodes.description: When Lisa learns Springfield's anthem was stolen from another town she writes a replacement. Meanwhile, Homer learns the meaning of "Wide Pride".

episodes.title: My Fare Lady
episodes.description: When Marge gets fed up with the thankless duty of chauffeuring her kids around, she begins working as a driver for a transportation app service. Meanwhile, Homer bankrupts Moe when he watches over the bar for one night, which forces Moe to take a temporary job as a janitor at the nuclear plant.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the first major TV series to directly reference the rise of ride-sharing apps like Uber and Lyft, positioning The Simpsons as an early commentator on the gig economy phenomenon.

episodes.title: The Princess Guide
episodes.description: Homer is put in charge of keeping Princess Kemi out of trouble while her father, a Nigerian king, works out a uranium deal with Mr. Burns.

episodes.title: Sky Police
episodes.description: Chief Wiggum is mistakenly delivered a military jet pack, which he gleefully accepts and uses in order to fight crime. But when the jet pack crashes into the church, the congregation, led by Marge, must resort to gambling and counting cards in order to collect money to repair the church.

episodes.title: Waiting for Duffman
episodes.description: When Duffman (guest voice Stacy Keach) undergoes hip replacement surgery and retires, the company sets up a reality show competition to find his replacement. Homer wins the competition, and because the job requires him to stay sober, he learns that beer isn't as necessary as he thought to have a good time.

guests.character: Howard K. Duff VIII  (voice) / Don Bookner (voice)

episodes.notes: Stacy Keach's casting as Duffman marked the character's first major speaking role with a celebrity voice actor, as previous appearances featured standard voice cast members.

episodes.title: Peeping Mom
episodes.description: Bart lies about being involved in a bulldozer crash, so Marge decides to follow him everywhere until he confesses. Meanwhile, Homer ignores Santa’s Little Helper when Flanders gets a new dog.

episodes.title: The Kids Are All Fight
episodes.description: When Homer gets an old film roll developed, the family takes a trip down memory lane to see the origins of how Bart and Lisa first started fighting with each other.

episodes.title: Let's Go Fly a Coot
episodes.description: The Simpsons learn about Grampa’s days in the Air Force, and Bart takes up smoking to impress Milhouse’s Dutch cousin, Annika.

episodes.title: Bull-E
episodes.description: After Bart gets bullied at the school dance, Marge convinces the town to pass anti-bullying legislation. Homer then gets arrested and sent to rehab for bullying Ned Flanders, only to subsequently leave rehab as a hero.

episodes.title: Mathlete's Feat
episodes.description: When a modernized Springfield Elementary has a technical meltdown, Lisa transforms it into a Waldorf school. Meanwhile, Groundskeeper Willie becomes the coach of the mathletes.

episodes.title: Every Man's Dream
episodes.description: Homer is diagnosed with narcolepsy, but instead of returning home with his prescription medication, he returns home drunk. He and Marge visit a marriage counselor and have a trial separation, during which Homer begins dating a 20-something.

guests.character: Employment Agent / Admitted Fraud #2 / Angry Woman (voice)

episodes.notes: This season 27 premiere marked the first time in the show's history that Homer and Marge actually separated and filed for divorce, rather than just threatening it.

episodes.title: Cue Detective
episodes.description: Scotty Boom, a Chew Network super-chef, challenges Homer to a smoke-off. But when someone steals Homer’s secret weapon – a smoker he bought off a mysterious man at a BBQ shack – Lisa and Bart are on the case.

episodes.title: Puffless
episodes.description: Upon learning that their father really died of lung cancer, Selma and Patty agree to quit smoking. When Selma relapses, an angry Patty moves in with Marge and Homer.

guests.character: Brandine Del Roy / Plopper / Dubya Spuckler (voice)

episodes.title: Halloween of Horror
episodes.description: When Lisa becomes scared at the Krustyland Halloween Horror Night, Homer is forced to take down his Everscream Terrors decorations. Halloween pop-up employees seek vengeance on Homer for causing them to lose their jobs, and rob his house while Homer and Lisa hide out in the attic. Homer and Lisa light up their stored holiday decorations to attract attention and stand up to the intruders.
episodes.notes: This marked the first time The Simpsons aired a non-anthology Halloween episode outside of the annual Treehouse of Horror format, breaking a 26-year tradition.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XXVI
episodes.description: In the annual fright fest Halloween episode, Sideshow Bob finally kills Bart, only to reanimate him; Homer wakes up with short-term memory loss; and Lisa, Bart, and Milhouse get super powers from radiation.

guests.character: Kumiko Nakamura / News Reporter / Dolph / Wendell Borton (voice)

episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which turns into a monster and eats them.
episodes.parody_of: Wanted, Memento, and Chronicle/superhero origin stories
episodes.notes: The first segment marks the rare occurrence of Sideshow Bob actually succeeding in killing Bart, fulfilling his decades-long quest that began in Season 1.

episodes.title: Friend with Benefit
episodes.description: Lisa makes a new pal at school named Harper, whose internet billionaire father becomes buddies with a different Simpson: Homer. Soon, though, Lisa becomes worried that Harper is a little too entitled.

episodes.title: Lisa with an 'S'
episodes.description: When Homer loses $5,000 at a poker game with Broadway legend Laney Fontaine, the only way he is able to settle the bet is if he loans Lisa to Laney for a month. Then, Laney turns Lisa into a show biz kid and Marge and Homer fear they made a mistake letting her go. So they head to New York to get her back.
episodes.parody_of: Broadway shows and show business culture

episodes.title: Paths of Glory
episodes.description: Lisa sets out to restore the tarnished reputation of Springfield’s first female inventor. To find her invention, she scours an abandoned asylum and a restaurant that caters to men. Bart joins Lisa at the asylum, stealing one of the homicidal patient’s notebooks, and bragging to the boys at school that he wrote the entries himself. This revelation causes Homer and Marge to believe he is a sociopath, which Bart decides to use to his advantage until things go too far.

episodes.title: Barthood
episodes.description: Bart’s coming of age story a la “Boyhood” chronicles his life from six years old to his time as an accomplished young man. Along the way, his tense relationship with Homer, an overly uncaring version of himself, and Lisa, who overshadows him in every way possible, shape Bart more than he realizes.
episodes.notes: This ambitious parody of Richard Linklater's "Boyhood" was filmed over several years to authentically capture the aging of Bart's voice actor Nancy Cartwright, making it one of the most technically complex episodes in the series' history.

episodes.title: The Girl Code
episodes.description: After a photo Marge posts on social media gets Homer fired, he returns to the best job he ever had, a dishwasher at a Greek restaurant. This same post inspires Lisa to code an app that predicts the real-life consequences of anything you post online, only to create sentient artificial intelligence.

episodes.title: Teenage Mutant Milk-Caused Hurdles
episodes.description: There is a new teacher at Springfield Elementary, and a captivated Bart tries everything to win her over. Meanwhile, Homer decides to buy a new brand of “milk,” which causes early puberty, both for Bart (augmenting his wooing powers) and Lisa (whose small bout with acne opens her up to the world of makeup and popularity).

episodes.title: Much Apu About Something
episodes.description: Sanjay passes his stake in the Kwik-E-Mart to his millennial son, Jay, who turns it into a hip, health food market against Apu’s insistence on tradition. Bart, whom Homer had convinced to give up pranking, is called upon to return to his trickster life and take down the new store.

episodes.title: Love Is in the N2-O2-Ar-CO2-Ne-He-CH4
episodes.description: Lonely Professor Frink uses science to turn himself into a man whom women find attractive. Then,  overwhelmed by the attention, he invents an algorithm to perfectly pair the lonely men and women of Springfield, realizing that scientific exploration is his true love. Meanwhile, Marge, Bart, and Lisa visit Grampa for Valentine's Day and try to rescue the old folks from a drug-induced hallucination that allows them to relive their happiest memories.

episodes.title: Gal of Constant Sorrow
episodes.description: After Bart accidentally sends a sweet homeless woman's cart into the river, he allows her to live in his closet for a small fee. When Lisa discovers that the woman is an incredible folk singer, she offers her own closet and plans a concert, only to be disappointed by Hettie's surprising substance abuse and violent nature. Meanwhile Homer tries to prove he's handy, but accidentally traps the cat in the walls of the house.
episodes.parody_of: Folk music culture and musician biographies

episodes.title: Lisa the Veterinarian
episodes.description: After performing CPR on a tased raccoon, Lisa discovers her calling to be veterinarian and becomes an intern at the local office, but learns a tough lesson when her neglected class hamster dies. Meanwhile, Marge moonlights as a crime scene cleaner for a little extra money, and the trauma of the gory scenes make her increasingly dead inside.

episodes.title: The Marge-ian Chronicles
episodes.description: Not feeling unique, Lisa signs up for the Mars One Space Colony – to Marge’s dismay. Then, Marge hires Bart to go through the tryout process with Lisa to make her want to quit.
episodes.parody_of: Mars One mission and space colonization programs
episodes.notes: This episode parodies the real Mars One project, which was actively recruiting civilian volunteers for a one-way trip to Mars when the episode aired, making it one of the show's most timely science fiction parodies.

episodes.title: The Burns Cage
episodes.description: After Smithers is crushed by Mr. Burns' lack of affection towards him, other characters attempt to find a boyfriend for him. Meanwhile, Milhouse competes against a new boy for the lead role in a school production of Casablanca, so he can act alongside his own unrequited love, Lisa.
episodes.notes: This episode marked the first time Smithers' romantic feelings for Mr. Burns were explicitly acknowledged and explored as a central plot, definitively confirming his sexual orientation after decades of hints and subtext.

episodes.title: How Lisa Got Her Marge Back
episodes.description: After getting into an argument, Marge takes Lisa on a touristy trip to Capital City, where, after attending a musical, they meet the show’s star who reminds Lisa how lucky she is to have such a great mom. Meanwhile, Bart is frustrated that everyone expects his pranks, so he teams up with Maggie to trick people.

episodes.title: Fland Canyon
episodes.description: Homer recalls a tense family vacation to the Grand Canyon with the Flanders family. At first, the Flanders’ perfection puts the families at odds, but when they get stranded and Ned and Bart go for help, the families finally bond.

episodes.title: To Courier with Love
episodes.description: "The Simpsons are going to..." Paris (well, Homer and Marge are), but in order to be able to afford the trip, Homer has to smuggle an exotic snake into France so it can be made into a gourmet meal.

episodes.title: Simprovised
episodes.description: After butchering a speech in front of all of his friends at work, Homer turns to improv comedy to gain back his confidence in his public speaking skills.  Meanwhile, Marge decides to rebuild Bart’s lackluster treehouse, but tensions rise when Bart questions her building skills. Also, in the episode’s last three minutes, Homer  appears LIVE for the first time on television (both for East and West Coast broadcasts) to answer fan questions.
episodes.notes: The first and only time Homer Simpson appeared live on television, with Dan Castellaneta performing unscripted dialogue in real-time for both East and West Coast broadcasts during the episode's final three minutes.

episodes.title: Orange is the New Yellow
episodes.description: Marge goes to prison for letting Bart play in a park unsupervised.
episodes.notes: This episode marked The Simpsons' first parody of a Netflix original series, reflecting the streaming platform's growing cultural dominance in 2016.

episodes.title: Monty Burns' Fleeing Circus
episodes.description: When a fire levels Springfield, Mr. Burns agrees to the Simpsons' pleas that he fund the town's rebuilding, on the condition that he gets to put on a variety show.

episodes.title: Friends and Family
episodes.description: Mr. Burns hires the Simpsons, except Homer, to be his virtual family; Homer becomes best friends with the girl next door.

episodes.title: The Town
episodes.description: After Homer catches Bart rooting for Springfield’s football rival, the Boston Americans, he plans a family “hate-cation” trip to Boston in an attempt to show Bart that Boston is a terrible city.
episodes.parody_of: Boston sports culture and New England stereotypes

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XXVII
episodes.description: Better late than never, The Hunger Games come to Springfield!
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which turns into a monster and eats them.
episodes.notes: This marks the first time a Treehouse of Horror episode directly parodied a contemporary blockbuster franchise still actively releasing films, with The Hunger Games trilogy having concluded just one year prior in 2015.

episodes.title: Trust But Clarify
episodes.description: Lisa and Bart investigate Krusty’s suspicious new "Krustaceans" candy. Meanwhile, Homer wants a promotion at the nuclear plant and asks Marge to help him dress the part, and news anchor Kent Brockman struggles to find his feet in the changing media world.

episodes.title: There Will Be Buds
episodes.description: Homer gets roped into coaching the kids' lacrosse team with Milhouse's dad, Kirk, who is in desperate need of a friend. When Kirk gets too clingy, Homer vents about how much of a loser he is. Kirk overhears his rant and disappears, right when the team needs him most: the championship game.

episodes.title: Havana Wild Weekend
episodes.description: When the Retirement Castle and the V.A. Hospital can’t solve Grampa’s health issues, the Simpsons decide to take a family trip to Cuba to get him cheap medical care.

guests.character: Howard K. Duff VIII  (voice) / Don Bookner (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode aired just eight days after Donald Trump's election victory, making it one of the first TV shows to depict Cuba during a period of uncertain U.S.-Cuba relations following the Obama administration's diplomatic thaw.

episodes.title: Dad Behavior
episodes.description: Homer finds a new app that makes his life much easier and outsources his father-son bonding. Meanwhile, Grampa finds that he is about to be a father again.
episodes.notes: The episode introduces an app-based parenting service that predates the real-world boom in AI-powered childcare and family management apps by several years, showing The Simpsons' continued ability to anticipate technological trends.

episodes.title: The Last Traction Hero
episodes.description: A workplace accident leaves Homer in a cast, and in a position to sue Mr. Burns. This leaves Marge unfulfilled, so she turns to an unexpected source for romance. Meanwhile, Lisa is made "Bus Monitor" and tries to keep the peace.

episodes.title: The Nightmare After Krustmas
episodes.description: Krusty tries to impress his daughter as they spend Christmas with the Simpsons. Meanwhile, Reverend Lovejoy seeks converts after church attendance plummets, and Maggie is haunted by a spooky Christmas toy.

episodes.title: Pork and Burns
episodes.description: When Marge becomes obsessed with a Japanese style of living, the Simpsons must part with any item that no longer brings them joy. For Homer, this means finding a new home for the family pig. For Lisa, it means saying goodbye to her beloved sax

guests.character: Employment Agent / Admitted Fraud #2 / Angry Woman (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the first major American animated series to parody Marie Kondo's "KonMari Method," airing just two years before her Netflix series would make the decluttering guru a global phenomenon.

episodes.title: The Great Phatsby (1)
episodes.description: Mr. Burns tries to relive his glory days and crosses paths with a mysterious music mogul. After being conned by him and reduced to bankruptcy, Mr. Burns seeks revenge on the Music Producer with the help of Homer, Bart, rapper Jazzy James and the mogul's ex-wife, Praline. Also, Lisa is courted by the richest kid in town, Smithers begins a perilous journey and Marge opens an obscure boutique store.

guests.character: Hermes Conrad / Auctioneer / Male party guest (voice)

episodes.title: The Great Phatsby (2)
episodes.description: Mr. Burns tries to relive his glory days and crosses paths with a mysterious music mogul. After being conned by him and reduced to bankruptcy, Mr. Burns seeks revenge on the Music Producer with the help of Homer, Bart, rapper Jazzy James and the mogul's ex-wife, Praline. Also, Lisa is courted by the richest kid in town, Smithers begins a perilous journey and Marge opens an obscure boutique store.
episodes.notes: This hour-long musical episode featured guest stars Keegan-Michael Key as hip-hop mogul Jay G and Common as rapper Jazzy James in a loose parody of The Great Gatsby. The ambitious production marked one of the show's most elaborate musical episodes, though it received mixed reviews and struggled to capture the cultural impact of earlier musical specials.

episodes.title: Fatzcarraldo
episodes.description: All of the fast food restaurants in Springfield go healthy, forcing Homer to turn to the last bastion of greasy food for comfort.
episodes.notes: This episode parodies Werner Herzog's 1982 film "Fitzcarraldo," in which Klaus Kinski's obsessed opera lover drags a steamship over a mountain in the Amazon. The title "Fatzcarraldo" cleverly combines Homer's weight obsession with Herzog's notoriously difficult production about moving a massive vessel through impossible terrain.

episodes.title: The Cad and the Hat
episodes.description: Bart deals with his guilt after betraying Lisa; Springfield is in awe of Homer when he is revealed to be a chess savant.

episodes.title: Kamp Krustier
episodes.description: Bart and Lisa return home from Kamp Krustier early, interrupting Homer and Marge's romantic encounters; Bart and Lisa confront a source of trauma.

episodes.title: 22 for 30
episodes.description: Bart goes from delinquent with detention to the star basketball player at Springfield Elementary. Lisa covers Bart’s success for the school paper, and Homer becomes the team’s coach. Things go awry when Bart gets involved with the mafia

episodes.title: A Father's Watch
episodes.description: Marge turns to a series of parenting experts for advice when she becomes worried that Bart is destined for failure; Homer decides to open a trophy store; Grampa gives Bart a watch coveted by Homer.

episodes.title: The Caper Chase
episodes.description: Disappointed in Yale, Mr. Burns decides to start his own for-profit university and Homer is hired as a professor
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the latest-season appearances by Mr. Burns as a central character driving the main plot, reflecting the show's continued relevance in satirizing contemporary issues like predatory for-profit education scandals.

episodes.title: Looking for Mr. Goodbart
episodes.description: Bart gets in trouble on Grandparents Day at school and is forced to hang out with Skinner's mother as punishment. After spending time with various grandmas in the neighborhood, he learns the benefits of hanging out with the elderly, but eventually recognizes the dangers of taking advantage of them. Meanwhile, Peekimon Get has taken over the town of Springfield.
episodes.notes: This episode marked The Simpsons' timely parody of the Pokémon Go phenomenon, arriving less than a year after the mobile game's explosive global launch in July 2016.

episodes.title: Moho House
episodes.description: Mr. Burns makes a bet with his old friend that nothing can break Homer and Marge apart. But that's where Moe comes in.

episodes.title: Dogtown
episodes.description: After a successful animal rights campaign, all dogs in Springfield are allowed to go where they want and do what they want. Pack mentality quickly kicks in and the dogs of Springfield assert their dominance by threatening the people.

guests.character: Employment Agent / Admitted Fraud #2 / Angry Woman (voice)
